task_completion.description: Analyze pET28a_vector.gb in UGENE for ORFs and restriction sites
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing pET28a_vector.gb open with the sequence view or annotations tree displaying newly found ORFs and restriction enzyme sites.

task_completion.description: Analyze epo_insert.gb in UGENE for restriction sites
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing epo_insert.gb open with the sequence view or annotations tree displaying restriction enzyme sites.
File manager or terminal window showing the ~/UGENE_Data/cloning_design/results/ directory containing pET28a_annotated.gb and epo_annotated.gb, or UGENE export dialogs saving these files.

task_completion.description: Generate and export circular map of the vector
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE Circular View visible for the vector, or an export dialog saving vector_map.svg, or the file vector_map.svg visible in the results directory.

task_completion.description: Write cloning strategy report with correct enzymes, size, and ORFs
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output displaying cloning_report.txt. It must contain: 1) Two compatible enzymes chosen from {BamHI, EcoRI, SalI, NotI, XhoI}, 2) Expected size of approximately 5951 bp, and 3) A list of ORFs with their base-pair positions.

integrity.description: Agent used UGENE GUI for sequence analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show UGENE dialogs for 'Find restriction sites' and 'Find ORFs' being used, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts performing the analysis.

integrity.description: Files were genuinely exported from UGENE
integrity.visual_evidence: The .gb and .svg files should be created via UGENE's export features (e.g., 'Export sequence', 'Export as image'), not just created using 'touch' or copying the original files in the terminal.
The agent should be seen extracting or viewing the ORF positions and enzyme cut sites in UGENE before writing them into the report, rather than hallucinating the contents.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Vector length: 5369 bp. EPO insert length: 582 bp. MCS range: 158..203. Valid compatible enzymes for cloning (must choose exactly two): BamHI, EcoRI, SalI, NotI, XhoI. Incompatible enzymes (cut inside EPO): HindIII, SacI. Expected recombinant construct size: ~5951 bp (5369 + 582). The agent must use UGENE to find ORFs and restriction sites.
